name: Bemposta Dam and Fermoselle Loop
name_original: Fermaselle – vuelta por la Presa de Bemposta – Parque Natural de Duero Internacional
description: This moderate 32.3-mile road cycling route through Duero International Natural Park offers dramatic scenery and significant elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling around Villar Del Buey offers diverse terrain within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park, characterized by the dramatic canyons of the Douro River. The landscape features deep ravines, rolling hills, and vineyards, providing varied elevation changes for cyclists. This region, part of the Meseta Ibérica biosphere reserve, includes valleys and pastures alongside its more rugged sections. The area's geographical variety supports routes ranging from moderate to those requiring very good fitness. Best road cycling routes around Villar Del Buey The most popular road cycling route is Villar del Buey – return via Bermillo de Sayago and Almeida de Sayago, a 27.8 miles (44.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the local villages of the Sayago region. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Almendra Reservoir and Fermoselle Loop, a moderate 31.9 miles (51.5 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain as it circles the Almendra Reservoir and passes through Fermoselle. Local road cyclists also love the Embalse de Almendra – return through the villages of Sayago, a 39.1 miles (62.9 km) trail leading through the villages surrounding the Almendra Reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 33 minutes. Road cycling around Villar Del Buey is defined by the Douro River canyons, rolling hills, and traditional villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes with substantial climbs. The routes in Villar Del Buey are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 60 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villar Del Buey's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Villar del Buey?
A: Road cycling around Villar del Buey offers a diverse landscape within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park. You'll encounter dramatic canyons carved by the Douro River, rolling hills, and vineyards. The region, part of the Meseta Ibérica biosphere reserve, also features valleys and pastures, providing varied elevation changes for cyclists.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Villar del Buey area?
A: There are over 18 road cycling routes available around Villar del Buey, catering to various fitness levels and preferences.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Villar del Buey?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025790088/villar-del-buey-return-via-bermillo-de-sayago-and-almeida-de-sayago" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villar del Buey – return via Bermillo de Sayago and Almeida de Sayago&lt;/a&gt; route is classified as easy and explores local villages of the Sayago region.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For more experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 4 difficult routes in the area. While specific names aren't highlighted as 'difficult' in the provided data, routes with significant elevation gain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026436189/almendra-reservoir-and-fermoselle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almendra Reservoir and Fermoselle Loop&lt;/a&gt;, offer substantial climbs and are ideal for those with very good fitness.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I visit along the road cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in stunning viewpoints. You can find spectacular panoramic views at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4791099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Staircase Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; (Mirador de las Escaleras) in Fermoselle, offering vistas of rolling hills and vineyards. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Barrancas&lt;/a&gt; provides a breathtaking view of the Douro canyon and is excellent for birdwatching.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites to see while cycling?
A: Yes, the area offers glimpses into its rich heritage. You might encounter traditional Sayaguesa houses in villages, and historical landmarks like 'Los Potros' (old horse stocks) and 'Las Portaladas' (porticoes). The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442594" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgen del Castillo&lt;/a&gt;, near the Douro River, combines spiritual interest with scenic beauty.

Q: Can I find routes that circle around the Almendra Reservoir?
A: Yes, the Almendra Reservoir is a prominent feature in many routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025474527/embalse-de-almendra-return-through-the-villages-of-sayago" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Almendra – return through the villages of Sayago&lt;/a&gt; route leads through the villages surrounding the reservoir, offering picturesque views.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Villar del Buey?
A: The road cycling routes in Villar del Buey are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain and the engaging experience provided by the combination of challenging ascents and scenic descents through natural parks.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Sayago region's villages?
A: Absolutely. Many routes are designed to showcase the charming villages of the Sayago region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025794402/sayago-castles-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sayago Castles Route&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, allowing you to explore the local architecture and cultural landscape.

Q: What is the typical distance of road cycling routes in this area?
A: The road cycling routes around Villar del Buey vary in distance. You can find routes ranging from approximately 27.8 miles (44.7 km) for easier rides to longer options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025794402/sayago-castles-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sayago Castles Route&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 52.2 miles (84 km).

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Bemposta Dam?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023175510/bemposta-dam-and-fermoselle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bemposta Dam and Fermoselle Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that takes you past the Bemposta Dam, offering another scenic highlight of the region.
tips: Tranquil rural cycling route in the Arribes del Duero Natural Park Spectacular views of the Duero River curving the border landscape between Portugal and Spain.
From this road you can enjoy two spectacular views, one of the Duero River below and the other of the houses hanging on the granite rock cliffs. Fernoselle is a quaint little historic art town.
On the course of the Duero River is the Bemposta Dam, which at the same time marks the border between Spain and Portugal. The name is due to the Portuguese people who are very close to the dam.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Fermaselle – tour around the Bemposta Dam – Duero International Natural Park route?
faqs.answer: This road cycling tour is approximately 51.9 kilometers long and typically takes around 2 hours and 35 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your cycling speed, fitness level, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Fermaselle – tour around the Bemposta Dam route is rated as moderate. While it involves significant elevation changes, with about 755 meters of ascent, it's designed for road cycling. Some sections in the wider region can be challenging with unpaved segments, but this specific Komoot tour is optimized for racebikes, suggesting mostly paved surfaces. Riders should have good fitness for the climbs and descents.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: You can expect a varied and scenic terrain. The route primarily follows paved roads suitable for racebikes, but be prepared for winding roads with demanding climbs and exhilarating descents. The landscape features dramatic river views, deep valleys, and hillsides covered in vineyards, olive groves, and almond orchards. While the Komoot tour is for racebikes, some regional routes may have unpaved sections, so always check the specific route details.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Fermaselle – tour around the Bemposta Dam route?
faqs.answer: This tour offers several notable sights. You'll get a fantastic View of the Duero River from Arribes del Duero Natural Park, pass by the historic Castle of Fermoselle, and cycle near the impressive Bemposta Dam. The route also takes you through traditional villages and offers glimpses of the region's rich cultural heritage.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While a specific trailhead parking lot isn't detailed, Fermoselle is a good starting point for this loop. You can typically find parking within the town of Fermoselle, which serves as a gateway to the Duero International Natural Park. Look for public parking areas or street parking that is permitted.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating, significant elevation changes, and 51.9 km distance, this specific route is generally better suited for cyclists with some experience and good fitness. Beginners or families with young children might find the ascents and overall distance challenging. There may be shorter, flatter routes available in the wider Duero International Natural Park for less experienced riders.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Fermaselle – tour around the Bemposta Dam route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. Spring also brings blooming wildflowers, while autumn offers beautiful fall foliage.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle in the Duero International Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, you do not typically need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to cycle this route within the Duero International Natural Park. The park is a protected natural area, but access for activities like cycling on established routes is generally free and open to the public. Always respect local regulations and signage.

faqs.question: Is the Fermaselle – tour around the Bemposta Dam route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route, bringing dogs along is generally not recommended unless they are safely secured in a bike trailer designed for pets. If you plan to stop and explore towns or natural areas, always check local regulations regarding dogs, especially within the natural park, where leash rules and restricted areas may apply.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with several other official trails. You will find sections that overlap with the Ruta del Duero (covering about 24% of the route), and smaller portions of Etapa 30: Fornillos de Fermoselle – Pinilla de Fermoselle, Etapa 32: Fermoselle – Trabanca, and Etapa 31: Pinilla de Fermoselle – Fermoselle. It also touches upon the [CIMA ZM01] Alto de Fermoselle and Mirador de las Escaleras.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through Fermoselle, a historic town where you can find cafes, restaurants, and other amenities. While the specific Komoot tour details don't list every refreshment stop, you can expect opportunities for food and drink in Fermoselle and potentially in other small villages you pass through. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer stretches between towns.

waypoints.name: View of the Duero River from Arribes del Duero Natural Park
